title: AI as Search: Problem Solving
description: How AI systems formulate problems as search and solve them with breadth-first, depth-first, and A* search over a state graph.
objectives: Formulate a problem as a search problem: initial state, actions, transition model, goal test, and path cost
Explain how a search tree is built by expanding nodes, and why an explored set avoids revisiting states
Trace breadth-first search and depth-first search by hand on a small graph, and explain which one guarantees the fewest-step path
Explain the idea of a heuristic function and trace greedy best-first search on a small weighted graph
Trace A* search by hand, computing f(n) = g(n) + h(n), and explain informally why an admissible heuristic (one that never overestimates) keeps A* optimal

lessons.title: Search problems: states, actions, and goals
